text: Managing a team effectively requires a balance of several key skills. First, clear communication is essential, as team members need to understand their goals and responsibilities. Delegation involves assigning tasks based on each person's strengths, which helps distribute workload fairly. Regular feedback, both positive and constructive, helps team members improve and feel valued. Motivation can be maintained by recognizing achievements and creating a supportive environment. When conflicts arise, swift resolution through open discussion prevents issues from escalating. By focusing on these areas, a manager can build a cohesive team that works together productively.
